title: Sick Leave Policy and Reporting Absences
text:
Q: What is the sick leave policy and how do I report an absence?
A: Our sick leave policy allows employees to take time off when they are unwell or unable to perform their duties due to illness. Employees are entitled to up to 10 days of paid sick leave per calendar year. To report an absence, you must notify your direct supervisor as soon as possible, ideally at least one hour before your scheduled start time. You can do this via email or phone call. If your absence extends beyond three consecutive days, a doctor's note may be required upon your return. For any questions regarding your sick leave balance or additional support, please contact the HR department at hr@company.com. Remember to keep your supervisor updated on your recovery and expected return date.
